I've verified your identity, Chen. I found two tablets:

1. **Order #W3069600** — Tablet, 8-inch, 64GB, silver — $938.92 (paid via Mastercard ending 2732)
2. **Order #W9571698** — Tablet, 8-inch, 128GB, gold — $989.70 (paid via gift card)

The pricier one is the 128GB gold tablet at $989.70. However, since that order was paid with your gift card, the refund can only go to the original payment method (gift card) or another existing gift card — it cannot be refunded to your credit card per our policy.

Would you still like to return the pricier tablet with the refund going to your gift card? Or would you prefer to return the other tablet ($938.92) which was paid by credit card and can be refunded to your card?

Wait, seriously? I can’t get the refund to my credit card for the more expensive one? That’s so frustrating! I really wanted that money back on my card, not a gift card. Ugh.

Fine, if that’s the only way, just refund it to my gift card then. Let’s get this over with—I want to return the gold 128GB tablet. What do I need to do next?
